I read the prompt as requiring a submission in the fixed tame format: first generators sigma,tau, implicit tame relation œÑœÉ=œÑ2, later generators in the pro-2 core, andZ -exponents represented modulo M=85667662080.The prior log‚Äôs only still-live candidate is the dynamic tame-inertia word variables sigma,tau,x,y definitions nx = (x*tau^50932630783)^40491355905 ny = (y*tau)^40491355905 relations x*sigma^45176306175*x*sigma^40491355905*x^-1*nx*ny^sigma*y^-1i.e.r=xœÉ‚àíœÄxœÉœÄx‚àí1(xœÑ‚àíŒ∑)œÄ ((yœÑ)œÄ)œÉy‚àí1,œÄ=œÄ2,Œ∑=2œÄ,with œÄ‚â°40491355905, ‚àíœÄ‚â°45176306175, and ‚àíŒ∑‚â°50932630783(modM). This is the candidate recorded in the prior log after the earlier scalar, plain-œÄ2, and fixed-œÑ‚àí2 proposals were refuted by finite-quotient tests.The first thing I checked is a recurring algebraic instability in Turns 28‚Äì37: the sign-cover geometric series was repeatedly written in both directions. The correct identity is the finite/geometric-series one. If t is the deck involution of the sign cover, œÑ‚Ü¶Œ±ÓÄÝ=1 is a nontrivial odd tame-inertia character, andNa (u)=(uœÑa)œÄ,then its Fox coefficient isDa=j=0‚àëœÄ‚àí1(tŒ±a)j=1‚àítŒ±a 1‚àí(tŒ±a)œÄ=1‚àítŒ±a1‚àít.

Here (tŒ±a)œÄ=t, because œÄ‚â°1(mod2) and œÄ‚â°0(mode). Thus the denominator 1‚àítŒ±a is a unit, while 1‚àít is radical. So Da is radical, and the coefficient of the correcting factor x‚àí1(xœÑ‚àíŒ∑)œÄ is ‚àí1+D‚àíŒ∑, a unit. This preserves the prior conclusion that nontrivial odd tame-character blocks contribute only unit Smith pivots, but the fraction must be written in this direction.The trivial tame-inertia block also has a direction issue. With T the œÉ-cycle operator and s‚â°œÄ(modf), the mod-2 linear relation is(1+T‚àís)X+(1+T)Y=0.

For f=2a m, m odd, one has s‚â°1(mod2a), s‚â°0(modm). If f is even, s is odd and 1+T‚à£1+T‚àís. Therefore the valid elimination is

Y=P(T)X+YÀâ,P(T)=1+T1+T‚àís,up to the harmless convention T‚ÜîT‚àí1. With this P,h(T)=1+P(T)+P(T‚àí1)satisfies, for Œ∂f=1, Œ∂ÓÄÝ=1,(1+Œ∂)h(Œ∂)=Œ∂‚àís+Œ∂s+1=Œ∂‚àís(1+Œ∂2s+1).Since 2s+1 is odd and congruent to 1(modm), gcd(f,2s+1)=1. Hence h has no nontrivial f-th-root zero. So the prior nonsingularity conclusion survives, but only with this quotient direction.My new computation addresses the exact next task left by Turn 37: the candidate‚Äôs class in the first mixed even-f ambiguity space for (e,f)=(3,2). LetŒî3,2‚âÉS3=‚ü®œÑ,œÉ‚à£œÑ3=œÉ2=1,¬ÝœÉœÑœÉ‚àí1=œÑ2‚ü©.

Use representatives œÑiœÉj. PutAi=XœÑi,Bi=XœÑiœÉ,S=œÉ2,i‚ààZ/3.For the degree-‚â§2 calculation, it is enough to use the finite approximation œÄ‚â°9, since 9‚â°1(mod8) and 9‚â°0(mod3).After Reidemeister‚ÄìSchreier rewriting and linear reduction, the clean invariant generator isb=Y1B0 where Y1=YœÑ0. In additive H1 -notation the Y-classes areYœÑi=b+Bi,YœÑiœÉ=b+Ai.

This realizesL1‚âÉF2[S3]‚äïF2 b‚äïF2S,and the remaining quadratic Demu≈°kin initial relation isq=i‚ààZ/3‚àë(Ai[2]+Bi[2])+[b,S].Now let Œ± be the quotient Frobenius action induced by conjugation by œÉ. Solving five of the six degree-2 Schreier relators to eliminate the Y-generators‚Äîany choice of the omitted relator gives the same answer modulo the remaining q‚ÄîI get Œ±(b)b‚àí1‚â°Z0 in¬ÝU2/U3,whereZd=i‚ààZ/3‚àë[Ai,Bi+d],d=0,1,2.

The b[2]and S[2]coefficients are 0, and the individual Z-coefficients are(b[2],S[2],Z0,Z1,Z2)=(0,0,1,0,0).This is a real mixed-tame datum. In Turn 37‚Äôs more conceptual split into the unramified-looking lineN=Z0+Z1+Z2 and two augmentation-zero mixed lines, e.g.M1=Z0+Z1,M2=Z0+Z2,the equality Z0=N+M1+M2 shows that the live candidate selects both mixed augmentation-zero directions nontrivially, not just the pure unramified direction. This does not refute the candidate, but it sharply specifies what the arithmetic comparison must now match in the first genuinely mixed case.So the live candidate is stronger than the earlier proposals, but it is still not a proved presentation. The missing theorem is no longer ‚Äúcheck more abelianizations‚Äù; it is a dyadic formation/rigidity comparison of the exact filtered (œÉ,œÑ)-operator action with arithmetic. The prior log already notes that the candidate is not yet a proof because the exact filtered operator action must be identified with the arithmetic action over all finite tame subextensions, not merely via abelianization, cup product, sign kernels, or cyclotomic branch tests.

ESTABLISHED: The live dynamic candidate remains unrefuted. I settled two formula directions:Da=1‚àítŒ±a1‚àít,P(T)=1+T1+T‚àís (up to T‚ÜîT‚àí1). New this turn: in the mixed quotient (e,f)=(3,2), with Ai=XœÑi, Bi=XœÑiœÉ, b=Y1B0, the candidate‚Äôs first filtered Frobenius class is Œ±(b)b‚àí1‚â°Z0=i‚àë[Ai,Bi]‚ààU2/U3.

Equivalently, relative to N=Z0+Z1+Z2, Z0+Z1, Z0+Z2, both mixed augmentation-zero components are nonzero.

GAP: This is still not a complete presentation of GQ2. The arithmetic side must be shown to have the same mixed (3,2) filtered Frobenius class Z0, and then the comparison must be extended to the full filtered operator action for all even-f tame quotients.

NEXT: Compute the corresponding arithmetic class over the tame S3 -extension of Q2 using explicit Kummer/Witt data through U/U3 or U/U5. The immediate target is to verify whether arithmetic Frobenius also gives Œ±(b)b‚àí1=Z0, including the two nonzero mixed augmentation-zero components.[BREAKTHROUGH]
